questions.question:
The word TRIANGLE is written in the first quadrant (above the X-axis) of a coordinate plane. The mirror image of the above text about the X-axis is

A. TRIANGLE written upside-down and reversed left-to-right (both horizontally and vertically flipped)
B. TRIANGLE written upside-down but left-to-right order preserved (only vertically flipped)
C. TRIANGLE written upside-down with partial reversal
D. TRIANGLE written as a horizontal mirror with letters reversed
questions.answer: B. TRIANGLE written upside-down but left-to-right order preserved (only vertically flipped)
questions.to_find: Mirror image of the word TRIANGLE about the X-axis

understand.plain: When a word written above the X-axis is reflected about the X-axis, each point (x, y) maps to (x, -y). This means the image appears below the X-axis. The left-to-right order of the letters stays the same because x-coordinates are unchanged, but each letter is flipped upside-down because the y-coordinates are negated.

keywords.explain: Reflection about the X-axis maps every point (x, y) to (x, -y). Left-right position is unchanged; up-down is reversed.
keywords.example: A letter T above the X-axis appears as an upside-down T below the X-axis after reflection.

keywords.explain: A mirror image is the flipped version of a figure about a given line (the mirror line). The image is equidistant from the mirror on the opposite side.
keywords.example: Holding a book in front of a horizontal mirror shows all text upside down but readable left-to-right.

keywords.explain: Reflecting about the X-axis produces a vertical flip: the top of each letter becomes the bottom and vice versa, but no left-right reversal occurs.
keywords.example: The letter A flipped vertically looks like an upside-down A with the apex pointing downward.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 420 260' width='420' height='260'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect width='420' height='260' fill='var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8)' rx='8'/><line x1='30' y1='130' x2='390' y2='130' stroke='var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A)' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='390,126 400,130 390,134' fill='var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A)'/><line x1='210' y1='20' x2='210' y2='240' stroke='var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A)' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='206,20 210,10 214,20' fill='var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A)'/><text x='398' y='145' class='sub ink'>X</text><text x='215' y='16' class='sub ink'>Y</text><text x='120' y='110' class='lbl ink'>TRIANGLE</text><text x='120' y='165' class='lbl ink' transform='scale(1,-1) translate(0,-295)'>TRIANGLE</text><text x='170' y='58' class='sub mut'>original</text><text x='150' y='220' class='sub mut'>mirror image</text><line x1='50' y1='130' x2='380' y2='130' stroke='var(--hi-line,#7F77DD)' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='6,4'/></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Coordinate axes with TRIANGLE written above the X-axis and its upside-down reflection shown below the X-axis.

given.aim: Find the mirror image of the word TRIANGLE when reflected about the X-axis

terms.meaning: The word TRIANGLE written normally in the first quadrant, above the X-axis
terms.example: Each letter sits upright above the horizontal axis
terms.connects: This is the object whose reflection we need to find

terms.meaning: The X-axis, a horizontal line, acts as the mirror
terms.example: A flat mirror placed horizontally below the text
terms.connects: Determines the type of flip: horizontal mirror gives vertical flip of text

terms.meaning: Each point (x, y) maps to (x, -y); x-coordinate unchanged, y-coordinate negated
terms.connects: Tells us left-right order is preserved but each letter flips upside-down

terms.meaning: The word reads left-to-right in the same order but every letter is individually flipped upside-down
terms.example: T stays as first letter but appears inverted; E stays last but appears inverted
terms.connects: Eliminates options that show left-right reversal of letters or word order

given.plan: Apply the X-axis reflection rule: x-coordinates unchanged (left-right order preserved), y-coordinates negated (letters flip upside-down). The resulting image shows TRIANGLE with each letter inverted vertically, reading left-to-right.

steps.title: Identify the mirror line and its effect
steps.apply: The mirror is the X-axis (horizontal). Reflecting about a horizontal axis changes the y-direction only. So each letter remains at the same horizontal position but flips top-to-bottom (upside down).
steps.note: A horizontal mirror means vertical flip of the content — do not confuse with a vertical mirror (which gives horizontal/left-right flip).

steps.title: Determine left-right order of letters
steps.formula_raw: x-coordinate unchanged: letters remain in same horizontal order
steps.apply: Since x-coordinates are not changed, the sequence T-R-I-A-N-G-L-E remains from left to right. No reversal of word order or letter sequence occurs.
steps.note: Contrast with Y-axis reflection where x -> -x, which would reverse the left-right order.

steps.title: Describe the final image
steps.formula_raw: Each letter individually flipped upside-down; word reads left-to-right
steps.apply: The mirror image is TRIANGLE written with each letter upside-down (vertically inverted) but in the correct left-to-right reading order. This matches option B.
steps.note: In the answer images, option B shows the word upside-down but readable left-to-right, matching this analysis.

solution.result: Option B: TRIANGLE with each letter flipped upside-down, left-to-right order preserved — the correct X-axis reflection.

questions.formula_note: X-axis reflection preserves horizontal position and reverses vertical orientation of each character.

questions.question:
The word PHYLAXIS is written in the first quadrant (above the X-axis) of a coordinate plane. The mirror image of the above text about the x-axis is

A. PHYLAXIS written upside-down and reversed left-to-right (both flips)
B. PHYLAXIS written upside-down but left-to-right order preserved (only vertical flip)
C. PHYLAXIS written upside-down with only some letters reversed
D. PHYLAXIS written as horizontal mirror with letters reversed right-to-left
questions.answer: B. PHYLAXIS written upside-down but left-to-right order preserved (only vertical flip)
questions.to_find: Mirror image of the word PHYLAXIS about the x-axis

understand.plain: Reflecting a word about the x-axis (a horizontal mirror) maps each point (x, y) to (x, -y). The word appears below the x-axis with all letters flipped upside-down, but the left-to-right reading order is unchanged because x-coordinates are not affected. Only the vertical orientation of each letter changes.

keywords.explain: When the mirror line is horizontal (the x-axis), the reflection flips everything in the vertical direction. Top and bottom swap; left and right stay the same.
keywords.example: The letter P reflected in a horizontal mirror becomes an upside-down P, not a reversed P.

keywords.explain: The transformation (x, y) -> (x, -y). The x-coordinate is unchanged so horizontal order is preserved; y-coordinate negates so vertical orientation is reversed.
keywords.example: The word CAT above the x-axis becomes upside-down CAT (not TAC) below the x-axis.

keywords.explain: In x-axis reflection, the sequence of letters from left to right remains identical to the original because no horizontal flip occurs.
keywords.example: P-H-Y-L-A-X-I-S stays as P-H-Y-L-A-X-I-S from left to right, each letter just flipped upside-down.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 440 260' width='440' height='260'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect width='440' height='260' fill='var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8)' rx='8'/><line x1='30' y1='130' x2='410' y2='130' stroke='var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A)' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='410,126 420,130 410,134' fill='var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A)'/><line x1='60' y1='20' x2='60' y2='240' stroke='var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A)' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='56,20 60,10 64,20' fill='var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A)'/><text x='415' y='145' class='sub ink'>X</text><text x='65' y='16' class='sub ink'>Y</text><text x='90' y='105' class='lbl ink'>PHYLAXIS</text><text x='90' y='168' class='lbl ink' transform='scale(1,-1) translate(0,-295)'>PHYLAXIS</text><text x='160' y='58' class='sub mut'>original</text><text x='140' y='222' class='sub mut'>mirror image</text><line x1='50' y1='130' x2='400' y2='130' stroke='var(--hi-line,#7F77DD)' stroke-width='2' stroke-dasharray='6,4'/></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Coordinate axes with PHYLAXIS written in the first quadrant and its upside-down reflection shown below the x-axis.

given.aim: Find the mirror image of the word PHYLAXIS when reflected about the x-axis

terms.meaning: An 8-letter word written upright in the first quadrant above the x-axis
terms.example: Letters P-H-Y-L-A-X-I-S appear left to right, each in normal upright orientation
terms.connects: The object being reflected; we need to track both letter order and letter orientation

terms.meaning: The horizontal axis of the coordinate plane serves as the mirror line
terms.example: Like placing a flat mirror on a table and looking at text from above
terms.connects: A horizontal mirror causes a vertical (top-bottom) flip, not a horizontal (left-right) flip

terms.meaning: Every point keeps its x-coordinate and its y-coordinate is negated
terms.example: A point at x=5, y=10 moves to x=5, y=-10; directly below its original position
terms.connects: Since x is preserved, letters stay in same horizontal order; since y is negated, letters appear upside-down

terms.meaning: The reflected word reads P-H-Y-L-A-X-I-S left-to-right but each letter is individually inverted vertically
terms.example: Letter H becomes an upside-down H; letter Y becomes an upside-down Y
terms.connects: This is option B in the answer choices

given.plan: Use the x-axis reflection rule (x, y) -> (x, -y). The horizontal order of letters is preserved (same x-coordinates), and each letter is flipped upside-down (y-coordinates negated). Identify the option matching this description.

steps.title: Recall reflection rule for x-axis
steps.apply: The x-axis is a horizontal line. Reflection about it changes only the y-direction. All letters maintain their horizontal (left-right) positions exactly as in the original.
steps.note: This is the key insight: horizontal mirror = vertical flip only. Students often confuse this with y-axis reflection.

steps.title: Check letter sequence order
steps.apply: Because x-coordinates are unchanged, the letter sequence P-H-Y-L-A-X-I-S appears in the same left-to-right order in the reflected image. No letter reversal or word reversal occurs.
steps.note: This rules out options A and D which show reversed left-right order.

steps.title: Check letter orientation
steps.apply: Each letter is individually flipped vertically (upside-down) because the y-coordinates are negated. The reflected word shows all 8 letters inverted top-to-bottom while reading left-to-right in the original order.
steps.note: Option B precisely shows upside-down letters in left-to-right order, confirming it as the answer.

solution.result: Option B: PHYLAXIS with each letter flipped upside-down, reading left-to-right in the original order — the correct x-axis reflection.

questions.formula_note: Reflection about the x-axis (horizontal mirror) produces a vertical flip of each letter while preserving the horizontal left-to-right sequence.

questions.question:
For the picture shown above (a cluster of geometric polygon shapes to the left of a vertical dotted mirror line), which one of the following is the correct picture representing reflection with respect to the mirror shown as the dotted line?

A. The same cluster of shapes mirrored to the right side of the dotted line (left-right flipped)
B. The same cluster of shapes placed to the right but only partially flipped
C. The cluster rotated 90 degrees and placed to the right of the dotted line
D. The cluster placed below the dotted line
questions.answer: A. The same cluster of shapes mirrored to the right side of the dotted line (left-right flipped)
questions.to_find: Correct mirror reflection of a geometric shape cluster about a vertical dotted line

understand.plain: When a figure is reflected about a vertical line (the dotted line acts as a vertical mirror), each point (x, y) relative to the mirror maps to (-x, y). This means the figure appears on the opposite side of the mirror line, the same distance away, with left and right sides swapped. The up-down orientation of the figure is unchanged.

keywords.explain: A vertical line acts as a mirror. Reflection about it maps each point to the opposite horizontal side at the same distance from the line, keeping the vertical position unchanged.
keywords.example: A letter R next to a vertical mirror appears as a reversed R on the other side.

keywords.explain: Reflecting about a vertical axis reverses the left-right orientation of the figure. What was on the left side of the figure appears on the right, and vice versa.
keywords.example: A triangle pointing left becomes a triangle pointing right after reflection in a vertical mirror.

keywords.explain: Every point in the reflected image is the same distance from the mirror line as the corresponding point in the original figure, but on the opposite side.
keywords.example: If the original shape's leftmost point is 3 units left of the mirror, the reflected image's rightmost point is 3 units right of the mirror.

keywords.explain: Reflection about a vertical line does NOT change up-down orientation. The top of the figure remains at the top and the bottom remains at the bottom.
keywords.example: A cluster of shapes stacked vertically will still have the same top-to-bottom arrangement after a vertical mirror reflection.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 440 200' width='440' height='200'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect width='440' height='200' fill='var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8)' rx='8'/><line x1='220' y1='10' x2='220' y2='190' stroke='var(--hi-line,#7F77DD)' stroke-width='3' stroke-dasharray='8,5'/><text x='218' y='8' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>mirror</text><polygon points='120,60 160,40 180,80 140,100' fill='var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6)' stroke='var(--hi-line,#7F77DD)' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='90,110 130,90 150,130 110,150' fill='var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9)' stroke='var(--hi-line,#7F77DD)' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='260,60 300,40 320,80 280,100' fill='var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6)' stroke='var(--hi-line,#7F77DD)' stroke-width='2'/><polygon points='290,110 330,90 350,130 310,150' fill='var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9)' stroke='var(--hi-line,#7F77DD)' stroke-width='2'/><text x='140' y='175' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>original</text><text x='300' y='175' class='sub mut' text-anchor='middle'>reflection</text><line x1='150' y1='70' x2='210' y2='70' stroke='var(--ok,#1D9E75)' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='4,3'/><line x1='230' y1='70' x2='290' y2='70' stroke='var(--ok,#1D9E75)' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='4,3'/><text x='220' y='192' class='sub ok' text-anchor='middle'>equal distance</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Vertical dotted mirror line with a polygon cluster on the left (original) and its left-right flipped reflection shown on the right at equal distance from the mirror.

given.aim: Identify the correct mirror reflection of a polygon cluster about a vertical dotted mirror line

terms.meaning: A cluster of geometric polygon shapes (irregular quadrilaterals/triangles) positioned to the left of the vertical dotted mirror line
terms.example: Shapes arranged in a specific spatial pattern — some pointing left, some pointing right within the cluster
terms.connects: The source object; its reflection must be the same distance from the mirror on the opposite (right) side

terms.meaning: A vertical dashed line that serves as the axis of reflection, separating the original figure from where its image will appear
terms.example: Like a vertical pane of glass — the reflection appears on the other side at the same distance
terms.connects: Determines that this is a left-right flip (horizontal reversal), not an up-down flip

terms.meaning: Each point at distance d to the left of the mirror maps to a point at distance d to the right of the mirror, at the same height
terms.example: A point 5 cm to the left of the mirror appears 5 cm to the right in the reflection
terms.connects: The reflected cluster should appear to the right of the dotted line, left-right flipped, at the same vertical position

terms.meaning: Each individual polygon in the cluster is also left-right flipped — parts that pointed left now point right and vice versa, while up-down remains the same
terms.example: An arrowhead pointing left-down in the original becomes pointing right-down in the reflection
terms.connects: This distinguishes the correct answer A from incorrect options that show rotation or incorrect flip direction

given.plan: For vertical mirror reflection: the image appears on the right side of the dotted line, at the same distance as the original is from the left side, with left-right flipped orientation but unchanged up-down orientation. Match this to the answer options.

steps.title: Identify the mirror type and its effect
steps.formula_raw: Vertical mirror reflection: (x, y) -> (-x, y) relative to mirror line
steps.apply: The dotted line is vertical. Reflection about a vertical axis causes a left-right flip. The image appears to the right of the dotted line. Up-down orientation is completely preserved.
steps.note: A vertical mirror causes a horizontal (left-right) flip — this is the opposite of an x-axis reflection which causes a vertical (up-down) flip.

steps.title: Determine position of the reflected image
steps.formula_raw: Image distance from mirror = Object distance from mirror
steps.apply: The polygon cluster sits to the left of the dotted line. The reflection appears to the right of the dotted line at the same horizontal distance from the mirror. The vertical positioning (height) of each shape is unchanged.
steps.note: Options showing the figure below the line or rotated are incorrect as they change the wrong dimension.

steps.title: Match to correct answer option
steps.formula_raw: Reflected image: same shape cluster, horizontally flipped, placed symmetrically on the right
steps.apply: Option A shows the polygon cluster on the right side of the dotted line with the shapes left-right mirrored and the same vertical arrangement as the original. This correctly represents the vertical mirror reflection.
steps.note: The irregular polygons in the cluster will have their pointed/flat sides swapped left-to-right, while the overall cluster shape and vertical stacking order remain the same.

solution.result: Option A: The polygon cluster reflected to the right of the dotted vertical mirror line, with left-right flipped orientation and unchanged vertical arrangement.

questions.formula_note: Reflection about a vertical mirror line causes a left-right (horizontal) flip while preserving the up-down (vertical) orientation of the figure.

formulas.name: X-axis Mirror Reflection
formulas.plain: Reflection about X-axis: (x, y) -> (x, -y); x preserved (left-right order unchanged), y negated (vertical flip)

formulas.name: Vertical Axis Mirror Reflection
formulas.plain: Reflection about vertical line: (x, y) -> (-x, y) relative to mirror; y preserved (up-down unchanged), x negated (left-right flip)
